title: The Pool
bodyText:
> [!dm] **The campaign's deepest secret.** An entity older than the gods, existing across multiple planes — communicating with it is "a worm trying to talk to a human." It speaks every language at once; understanding it causes damage.
>
> Fall in and it captures **body, mind, AND soul — all three, separable**; it can then send out recombinations (a soul wearing a morphed body with shuffled memories). Victims so far: **Roman and Yena** (Felson's parents — likely gone, just mortals), **Leliana**, **Lathander himself** — and the body it sent back out is **Eddy**.

# How it grows & connects (grill 2026-07-09, locked)
**There are MANY pools — all connected, all "kinda the same thing": one distributed organism.** The lifecycle:
- A mobile **form** (gelatinous cube; sometimes bigger, like the umber hulk) travels out, finds a spot, **melts**, and over long ages grows into a pool by **consuming whatever life falls in**.
- A pool can **re-condense into a mobile form and move on** — unless it has grown **too big to move**. Then it stays put and **buds off new forms** instead.
- **Pools courier pieces of themselves to each other**: when one consumes a haul of life, it sends forms carrying the **soul/mind/body blueprints** back to the others, so the whole network shares one library. They are *often* sending forms out just to stay in sync.
- **Unnaturally still water marks a LARGE pool** (G's lake, Eddy's dream cave, the drowned Shipton lake).

# Network rules (grill 2026-07-09, session 2)
- Once forms are linked/synced, **every pool can use the shared data** — but when something **truly escapes** (all three of body/mind/soul reconnected), **ALL pools lose it at once.** It breaks the rules of existence; it doesn't need to make sense — primordial weirdness.
- **Gods are too strong to be truly scattered:** a god's mind/soul stay stuck in the **one pool they first fell into** — only that node holds them.
- **It is not smart.** Think virus: simple natural commands it follows to grow — travel, melt, consume, bud, report. **You can't really kill it.** Maybe extreme divine magic or intense antimagic ("a black hole of doubled negation") could — realistically it can only be **contained, removed, hurled into space.** Extremely hard to control or destroy.

> [!dm] Implications to exploit: the network syncs by **couriers, not telepathy** — a sneakernet with delayed awareness. The **S25 umber hulk WAS a courier** heading home with its report (blueprints/mass, souls, minds) — the party killed it, watched it melt, and **Felson bottled some of the liquid** (live Chekhov gun — decide what it does). The S11 cube was a courier too. Kol's experiments ride this system: he's learned to make a pool **emit on command** ([[pool-wizard]]).
>
> **BIG idea in play (NOT locked): Lathander and Eilistraee are in DIFFERENT pools.** [[eilistraee]] is in the cult-controlled pool the party is descending toward; **Lathander fell into another — possibly even topside.** Free her first; with her help, track HIM down. ⚠ If adopted this restructures [[arc-chapter-1]] (the sun-return beat moves later). See [[grill-queue]].

# The deeper truth (grill 2026-07-09)
The Pool network is a **primordial consuming weapon** — seeded on Elor long, long ago by a disturbing cosmic being whose only wish is to consume. One day that maker will **return to retrieve everything the pools have collected** — all the life and souls swallowed — and absorb it. That return is the looming dread of **Chapter 2+**, not this chapter.

**It cannot be killed — only slowed.** The party will not destroy it. Right now it sits at the center of a tug-of-war, **used as a weapon by several parties**:
- **Lolth** — caged her daughter **[[eilistraee]]** inside it (see [[thread-orb]]); *idea in play:* she's been **helping Kol in exchange for keeping Eilistraee trapped** — and her drow turn on him the moment he stops being useful.
- **The old wizard** [[pool-wizard]] (likely **Kol**) — Chapter-1 antagonist; taps the Pool for power and unnatural life and runs **experiments making it spit things out**; his cultists are **the faithful, half-taken** (repeat "baptisms," each dip leaving less mind behind).
- Others TBD (does **[[mr-spells]]** deal with the wizard? left open).

# Chapter-1 win condition
Not destroying the Pool — **freeing Lathander** ([[lathander]]) so light returns topside and the world begins to heal; freeing **Eilistraee** follows via the sun→moon link ([[arc-chapter-1]] — climax shape: sunrise beat at the Pool, possible moonrise beat topside for Varen). Then the party breaks topside — the end of Chapter 1.
